questions.text: What is the primary message conveyed in the opening verses of Surah Hud?
questions.answer: The perfection of the Quranic verses and the command to worship none but Allah.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the provision of every living creature on earth?
questions.answer: Allah has guaranteed the provision (Rizq) for every creature and knows its dwelling and its repository.

questions.text: What was the challenge posed to the disbelievers regarding the Quran in this Surah?
questions.answer: They were challenged to bring ten invented surahs like it if they were truthful.

questions.text: What was the specific reply of Nuh (AS) when his people accused him of being a mere human like them?
questions.answer: He clarified that he does not possess the treasures of Allah, nor does he know the unseen, but follows clear proof from his Lord.

questions.text: What happened to the son of Nuh (AS) during the flood?
questions.answer: He refused to board the ark, seeking refuge on a mountain, and was drowned among the disbelievers.

questions.text: How did Nuh (AS) respond when Allah told him his son was 'not of his family'?
questions.answer: He realized that family ties are spiritual, based on righteous deeds and faith, rather than just blood.

questions.text: What was the central call of Prophet Hud to the people of 'Ad?
questions.answer: To worship Allah alone and seek His forgiveness so He would send abundant rain and increase their strength.

questions.text: What sign was given to the people of Thamud through Prophet Salih?
questions.answer: A miraculous She-Camel which was a test for them; they were commanded not to harm her.

questions.text: Why did Ibrahim (AS) feel fear when the angels visited him?
questions.answer: Because they did not reach out to eat the calf he had prepared for them, which was a sign of potential enmity or a grave mission.

questions.text: What glad tidings did the angels give to Ibrahim (AS) and his wife Sarah?
questions.answer: The birth of Ishaq (Isaac), and after him, Ya'qub (Jacob).

questions.text: How did the people of Lut (AS) respond to his warnings about their immorality?
questions.answer: They attempted to attack his guests and demanded he hand them over, showing their complete loss of shame.

questions.text: What was the specific punishment for the people of Lut (AS)?
questions.answer: Their city was turned upside down and stones of baked clay were rained upon them.

questions.text: What economic crimes were the people of Madyan (Prophet Shu'ayb) famous for?
questions.answer: Giving short measure and weight (cheating in trade) and spreading corruption.

questions.text: What is the 'Baqiyyatullah' mentioned by Prophet Shu'ayb?
questions.answer: That which remains with Allah (lawful profit/reward) is better for the believers if they are faithful.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the fate of Pharaoh and his followers on the Day of Resurrection?
questions.answer: Pharaoh will lead his people and proceed them into the Fire.

questions.text: What is the command given to the Prophet (SAW) in Verse 112 that is said to be the most difficult?
questions.answer: To stand firm (Istiqamah) as he has been commanded, along with those who have turned to Allah.

questions.text: What effect do good deeds have on evil deeds according to Verse 114?
questions.answer: Good deeds remove evil deeds (Innal hasanati yuth'hibnas sayyi'at).

questions.text: Why did Allah not destroy the towns while their people were reformers (Muslihun)?
questions.answer: Allah is never unjust to destroy a population while they are actively rectifying and doing good.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the stories of the Prophets mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: To make the heart of the Prophet (SAW) firm and to provide truth, an admonition, and a reminder for the believers.

questions.text: What is the final instruction in the Surah regarding trust in Allah?
questions.answer: To worship Him and rely upon Him (Tawakkul), for He is not unaware of what you do.
